disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began immediately on cold exposure (stepped outside into ~10°C air to put the bins out)
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when this started?
Did anything bring it on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started the second I went outside into the cold to put the bins out — it's about 10 degrees out there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral, symmetrical fingertip colour change: stark white, then dusky blue, now slowly reddening
trigger_question_examples: What colour did your fingers turn?
Is it one hand or both?
What do they look like now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both hands went dead white, then a horrible blue at the tips, and now the redness is only just starting to come back. It's both hands, all the fingers.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Attack is unusually prolonged (~45 minutes) and only partially resolving despite returning indoors
trigger_question_examples: How long has this been going on?
Has coming inside helped?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been going on nearly three-quarters of an hour now, and even though I came straight inside it still hasn't properly settled.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe, deep, throbbing ischaemic pain in the fingers; unable to use hands
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the pain?
Can you use your hands?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The pain is excruciating — a deep freezing throbbing ache — and I can't use my hands at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Established pattern of cold-induced fingertip blanching, usually milder and shorter-lived
trigger_question_examples: Has this ever happened before?
Do your fingers usually react to the cold?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My fingers do go white in the cold normally — I've been told it's Raynaud's — but it's never been this bad or lasted anything like this long.

trigger_question_examples: Do you have any other ongoing health issues?
Any heartburn or reflux?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I get a bit of heartburn / acid reflux now and again and take an antacid for it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Skin over the fingers has felt tight and looks shiny/stiff over recent months
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any change in the skin on your fingers or hands?
Does the skin feel tight or shiny?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, the skin on my fingers has felt a bit tight and looks shinier than it used to.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I hadn't really thought about it — I just assumed it was getting older.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No black discolouration, no open sores, ulcers or blisters on the fingertips
trigger_question_examples: Are any of your fingers turning black?
Are there any open sores, blisters or ulcers on your fingertips?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing's gone black and there are no sores or blisters — the tips are just bluish and numb.

trigger_question_examples: Do you smoke, or have you ever?
Any history of tobacco use?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain and no shortness of breath
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or trouble breathing?
Is it only your hands, or are you feeling unwell anywhere else?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain and no trouble breathing at all — it really is just my hands.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and cannot use hands, limiting ability to self-care and stay safe tonight
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone with you?
Are you able to manage on your own tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own, and with my hands like this I can't really do anything for myself right now.
